design_philosophy.core_loop: First-person exploration and combat with resource scarcity management. Player navigates a hostile starship, discovers audio logs for narrative context, defeats enemies using appropriate ammunition types, upgrades skills via cyber-modules, and manages limited health/nanite resources through vending machines and reconstruction devices.
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Resource scarcity (ammunition, health, nanites) against persistent environmental threats. Ammunition variety creates tactical depth—wrong ammo type reduces effectiveness, forcing players to choose carefully. Non-melee weapons degrade and require maintenance, adding durability pressure.
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Soldier fighting to contain a catastrophic genetic outbreak aboard a cyberpunk starship. Progressive skill development allows players to specialize (Marine/Navy/OSA paths) and gradually become more capable through character progression while uncovering the disaster's story.
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Hybrid action-RPG-survival-horror that treats FPS mechanics as a vehicle for character progression and world discovery rather than pure combat focus. Emphasizes narrative delivery through environmental audio logs and apparitions over cutscenes. Demands tactical ammunition selection and resource conservation, rejecting ammo abundance typical of 90s shooters. Starting career choice shapes initial capabilities but allows complete freedom in development, providing both structure and agency.

difficulty.lives_system: Quantum Bio-Reconstruction Machines provide revival within specific areas for 10 nanites cost. Outside these zones, death forces resume from last save point. No traditional lives system.
difficulty.continues_system: Save point based progression. Game ends on death outside reconstruction machine zones, requiring reload from last manual save.
difficulty.checkpointing: Manual save point system. Quantum Bio-Reconstruction Machines serve as localized checkpoints that consume in-game currency (nanites) to revive rather than offering free respawn.
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: High agency through multiple systems: (1) Starting career choice provides initial skill distribution (Marine/Navy/OSA) affecting early capabilities, (2) Cyber-module allocation allows complete skill customization post-character creation, (3) Weapon selection and ammunition type choice directly impact combat effectiveness, (4) Hacking minigame optional—ICE-picks provide automatic bypass, (5) Resource spending decisions (nanites for items vs. reconstruction) create strategic trade-offs.

progression.upgrade_system: Dual upgrade system: (1) Cyber-modules awarded for objectives/exploration, spent at cyber-upgrade units for skill enhancement, (2) O/S units provide one-time permanent character upgrades (e.g., health increases), (3) Nanite in-game currency purchases consumables at vending machines. Weapon degradation requires maintenance tool repairs, creating ongoing resource pressure.
progression.unlock_mechanism: Hacking system unlocks alternate areas and reduces vending machine prices. Keypads require minigame puzzle (connect three green nodes in straight row) or ICE-pick bypass. Skill progression unlocks new abilities tied to character class and cyber-module investment.
replayability_hooks: Three distinct starting careers (Marine/Navy/OSA) with different initial skill bonuses create varied early playstyles
Arbitrary skill allocation freedom allows vastly different character builds in subsequent playthroughs
Procedural-style ammunition scarcity encourages different weapon prioritization strategies
Multiple narrative elements (audio logs, apparitions) can be missed on first playthrough, supporting discovery replay
Hacking vs. ICE-pick decisions create branching resource management paths

world_building_techniques: Audio logs discovered throughout environment progressively reveal backstory and ship status
Ghostly apparitions encountered in-world provide narrative exposition and atmosphere
Environmental design of starship sections implies disaster progression and social hierarchy
Character career choice (Unified National Nominate military branches) establishes player perspective and fictional world detail
In-game items, vending machines, and technology (Quantum Bio-Reconstruction, cyber-upgrade units) communicate cyberpunk aesthetic and world state

faction_indicators: Unified National Nominate military organization with distinct branches (Marine, Navy, OSA)
Infected vs. uninfected crew divisions implied by genetic outbreak premise
danger_signals: Genetic infection outbreak devastating starship population
Enemy variety indicating different threat categories: organic enemies, mechanical foes, cyborgs, aliens (annelid-made weapons reference)
Weapon degradation and ammunition scarcity mechanics signal resource-constrained survival scenario
environmental_storytelling: Starship layout and accessible areas convey disaster progression and containment failures
Vending machines and reconstruction technology indicate pre-disaster infrastructure now repurposed for survival
Protocol droid encounters suggest automated systems still functioning in catastrophe context
Audio log distribution patterns suggest crew movement and final moments during outbreak

sources_used: wikipedia: System Shock 2 - Gameplay, Plot, and game design information
